INTERNAL MEMORANDUM
Client: Wyatt Industries, Inc. | Case ID: PSL-2024-0847
Assigned Attorney: Harvey Specter

---

MATTER OVERVIEW

This memo summarises the key facts, contractual provisions, and financial exposure in Wyatt Industries, Inc. v. Norwood Consulting Group, LLC, arising from a $4,200,000 Master Services Agreement (MSA) executed January 15, 2022. [PASSAGE 1]

PERFORMANCE FAILURES AND TERMINATION

Norwood's client satisfaction scores fell below the contractually required 80% threshold in two consecutive quarters: Q2 2022 (71%) and Q3 2022 (68%). [PASSAGE 2] Under Section 4.2 of the MSA, two consecutive quarters below threshold constitutes a material breach. [PASSAGE 3] Wyatt issued a formal underperformance notice on August 19, 2022, and subsequently invoked Section 7.1 to terminate the agreement on October 12, 2022. [PASSAGE 2]

Norwood disputed the termination on October 14, 2022, alleging the satisfaction scores were manipulated. A mediation attempt on November 5, 2022 failed. Wyatt filed suit in NY Supreme Court on January 8, 2024. [PASSAGE 2]

FINANCIAL EXPOSURE

Based on the financial summary in the case file: [PASSAGE 4]
- Total fees paid to Norwood through termination: $1,847,000
- Wyatt's disputed refund claim: $920,000
- Norwood's wrongful termination counterclaim: $2,353,000

NOTE ON LIABILITY CAP: Section 9.3 caps aggregate liability at total fees paid in the six months preceding the claim. [PASSAGE 3] As noted in file annotations, the $1,847,000 total paid likely caps Norwood's $2,353,000 counterclaim. This analysis should be confirmed as a priority action item.

UNCLEARNESS FLAGS

Page 2 of the source document contains a partially illegible scan (~4 lines). The initial phase delivery details from April 3, 2022 are only partially recoverable. This gap does not affect the core chronology but should be noted if Page 2 content becomes relevant at hearing.
